results.title: IRP1 [4Fe-4S] Cluster Occupancy as Feeding-Entrained Iron-Redox Chronostat
results.novelty_search_result: Nadimpalli 2024 notes cluster occupancy unmeasured; zero publications measuring IRP1 [4Fe-4S] oscillation over 24h. JCI 2026 BMAL1→ATP7A→Cu is distinct mechanism.
results.verdict_reason: Highest groundedness (8/8 claims verified). Builds on published gap (Nadimpalli 2024). Dual feeding-entrained mechanism Nernst-quantified. IRP2 KO separation test definitive. Modest IRP1 contribution (15-25%) honestly acknowledged.

results.title: CISD2 [2Fe-2S] as Redox-Gated ER-Mitochondrial Calcium Timer (Forward Direction Only)
results.novelty_search_result: Zero CISD2 × circadian publications on PubMed or Semantic Scholar. Novelty confirmed.
results.verdict_reason: Highest novelty (zero publications). Triple intersection (longevity × circadian × Fe-S). Redox midpoint error (-10mV is mitoNEET, not CISD2 ~0mV). Cluster stability vs sensitivity tension unresolved. Effect size extrapolated from KO.

results.title: CIA Pathway as LIP/ROS-Responsive Circadian Gate for Cytoplasmic Fe-S Proteome
results.novelty_search_result: Zero CIA pathway × circadian publications. Maio & Rouault 2022 JBC confirmed CIAO3 regulation by LIP/ROS but in acute context only.
results.verdict_reason: All claims verified. Novel application of CIAO3 data to circadian. Dual-input alignment elegant. ~20 proteins affected. Key weakness: acute perturbation data extrapolated to circadian-amplitude LIP oscillations (~10-15%).

results.title: Frataxin-Gated Fe-S Assembly via Mitochondrial LIP in FTMT-Negative Tissues
results.novelty_search_result: Zero publications on mitochondrial LIP circadian oscillation. FTMT absence in liver hepatocytes confirmed (Santambrogio 2007). Novel compartment argument.
results.verdict_reason: FTMT tissue-specificity argument novel and verified. Lill 2025 stoichiometric amplification real. Core mito LIP oscillation (20-30%) purely speculative. 'Unbuffered' overstated. FA carrier power optimistic. Borderline pass on FTMT novelty.

results.title: Conserved Fe-S Requirement in Clock Neurons — Drosophila to Mammalian SCN
results.novelty_search_result: Mandilaras 2012 only publication. Zero mammalian follow-up confirmed. 14-year gap verified.
results.verdict_reason: 14-year gap genuine. Multiple Fe-S genes converge in Drosophila. Reverse direction complementary. NFS1flox mice available. Mechanism is metabolic demand not specific. dCRY confound and neurodegeneration confounds unresolved.

results.title: NADPH→FDXR→FDX2 Electron Supply Chain as Circadian Fe-S Assembly Gatekeeper
results.novelty_search_result: Zero circadian FDX2/FDXR publications. But primary hypothesis (NADPH gating) quantitatively refuted by FDXR Km data.
results.verdict_reason: FDXR Km = 0.7µM (not 5µM estimated) makes NADPH arm quantitatively dead (<1% rate change at physiological NADPH). Double bottleneck collapses. Back-of-envelope refutes own hypothesis. Could reformulate around FDX2 cluster alone but as stated: FAIL.

meta_validation: 1 PASS, 4 CONDITIONAL_PASS, 1 FAIL. 17% QG kill rate (vs 0% in Session 006). All [GROUNDED] claims independently verified via 14 web searches. No vocabulary re-descriptions detected. No fabricated claims detected. Verdict distribution slightly generous (more CP than expected) but justified by genuine Cycle 2 improvement. Fe-S and circadian domain experts would likely agree with PASS/FAIL distribution.
